items.caption: It’s not the noise or the unpredictable summer timetables. It’s that your brain is still operating as if were term time. Something’s coming from @the_organised_method team in a couple of weeks that’s built exactly for this! More soon 👀
items.transcript: There's a lot of people panicking in my DMs, like what am I going to do with the house over the school holidays? Everybody just do it in a deep breath. Your house in the summer holidays, any busy period of your life, a completely different animal to your house during term time or when things are quieter. And we need to cut our cleaning cloth accordingly. If you are expecting to be maintaining your house to the same standard as when you've got more time and more energy, you're going to wear yourself out. This is maintenance season. All the good work that we've been putting in so far this year is going to stand you in really good stead. So we're just going to dip into that reserve a little bit, all of that contingency that we've built in, and we're just going to do one off over the summer to maintain, not stop altogether so that we move backwards, maintain the status quo. September, that's when the routines and the deep cleans and all of the good stuff start. We are in maintenance season.

items.caption: And it’s FREE 😜
items.transcript: Who wants to know the one thing that is going to guarantee to put you in a good mood for the rest of your day? In fact, I think that this is better than like an espresso first thing in the morning. It's calming down to a clean kitchen. It really is that simple. Because we've all been there, haven't we? Right before you might not have done the dishes so you've left them to soak and it instantly puts you on the back foot. Because if you flip it, you come down, everything's calm, you feel relaxed. You start the day off in a much better mood. So the message here is be your future friend. Do something for yourself today that is going to make tomorrow you much happier.

items.caption: Fully aware of the irony by the way 😬🙄
items.transcript: Cleaning content is lying to your brain. We all know, right, in real life, if you are going to give your living room or your kitchen a really good clean, it's going to take you some time. That's not the case on here though, is it? It takes 20 seconds, fast, sharp cuts, edited, satisfying click sound at the end. We all know it's edited. We're not stupid. Your brain is seeing this over and over again. Every video reinforces the fact that it should not be taking you as long in real life. And that's why you feel behind, because in a way, in a way, and I'm aware of the irony of me putting this video out, social media is gaslighting you.

items.caption: 50% untethered 😂
items.transcript: I actually know who I need to talk to about this in real life too, but I know that you'll understand it. So Ben is at his end of year six, right? So he's my youngest. He's about to finish primary school, about to go into senior school. And today is my last ever day of double school. So Mike and I have shared school runs for years, like kind of like 50-50. I'll do two and a half days and he'll do another two and a half days. Today is my last day of living my life between 10 and two. And I genuinely don't know what to do because I have had this scaffold for 14 years. I have been doing school runs, I might be wrong, for 14 years. I have got to learn a whole new way of doing this. And I am 50% excited and 50% completely untethered.

items.caption: When that routine gets taken away it’s normal to feel a bit all over the place ❤️
items.transcript: Some, it's no surprise to you whatsoever that I love a routine. I don't have a routine, I can feel a little bit wibbly wobbly. I know that over the coming days some of you might be in the same boat, especially if you've got kids that are just about to break up for the summer. You might be thinking, hold on a minute, I've been looking forward to it, I've been looking forward to not having to do the school run. It might feel great when initially that routine relaxes, but there may come a point, not necessarily, but there may come a point where you're a bit more overwhelmed than you thought you were going to, and I'm going to tell you why. When you're doing the school run in the morning, you've got a hard deadline. You have got a framework imposed externally on you and you have not got a choice, which means that the decisions have kind of been taken out of your hands. When that school run doesn't exist for the summer, that framework doesn't exist either. All of a sudden you are having to make more decisions, and to an already overwhelmed brain, that doesn't feel great, and it can result in you feeling overloaded, a little bit snappy, not as relaxed as you thought you were going to do. Forewarned is forearmed. If you know that that wobble is coming, you're like, do you know what, this is entirely normal.

items.caption: Live NOW in The Organised Method app ✨🙌#cleaningroutine #cleaningmotivation
items.transcript: If you want to reset the engine rooms of your house this week, then you are in luck because one has just gone live. It's an hour session. We're going to divide our time between three rooms, the kitchen, the living room, and the bathroom. Rooms that get the most use consistently in your house. If you haven't got a lot of time, but you need an intentional, highly focused guided productivity session, then go and check it out now. I just know it's going to become a firm favorite over the summer, so make sure you bookmark it to get some clean done.

items.caption: Proactive cleaning for the win 🙌✨
items.transcript: Can I tell you the most underrated strategy for keeping your house together? It's not a deep clean. It's not a full house reset. It's just doing the small stuff before it becomes a problem. I know that sounds stupidly simple. So things like making the bed before you go downstairs in the morning, not leaving the dishes to soak, it almost feels like it shouldn't be the answer, but it actually is. The mess is always going to come back because that is just what happens when people live in houses. It's not about having the perfect aesthetic, having a home that you feel like you can truly relax in. And we do that by staying one step ahead.

items.caption: Looking for help solving the root cause and not just the symptoms? That’s why I’m here 😍💅🏻
items.transcript: Look, you're probably going to really hate me for saying this, but if you love home organization content, that might be the reason why you have got so much clutter. This industry relies on you feeling overwhelmed. When you feel overwhelmed, you assume that you're failing. That makes you more susceptible to buying quick fixes. Quick fixes that don't actually fix anything at all because they're just like a sticking plaster and not dealing with the actual problem. And then you get stuck in this cycle where they don't work, so you buy more and you just keep accumulating clutter and you feel even more overwhelmed.

items.caption: And that’s why we prioritise the ‘feeling’ and not the aesthetic here 🩷🙌 because that is going to keep you very focused on what is worth your time, energy and money!
items.transcript: Now the home organization space, it's worth so much money, and it's built almost entirely feeling that you are not quite there yet. You just need to buy, you just need to do X, Y, Z. And even if you just think about the language, level up your space, transform your home, it's always just pressing that aspirational book.

items.caption: When you feel stressed and your life feels disorganised you are vulnerable to looking for quick fixes... BUT what if some of those are making the problem worse? This week’s YouTube episode all is about why you’re not really as disorganised as you think ... and I am going to prove it you! Love you! Gem xxx #cleaningroutine #theorganisedmum #mentalload
items.transcript: You're not actually disorganized. You've been told that you are repeatedly, so many times, that you've internalized it. People make money from you believing it. Someone shows you a really beautifully organized pantry and then you look at your kitchen and it doesn't look the same. And then automatically you feel behind because your life doesn't represent what you've just been shown. And this leaves you feeling like you're failing at something and that everybody else seems to have it all figured out. The problem isn't that you didn't have the right storage solution or the right mark. The problem is that you're doing an enormous amount of work with not enough time, not enough support. And there's a whole industry that profits from keeping you in that slightly off-kilter, chaotic space.

items.caption: ✨Ready for a bit of admin body doubling? 🙋🏻‍♀️See you in @the_organised_method
items.transcript: How many of you find it tricky to sit down and force yourself to do some admin? Oh, it's just a couple of emails, I'll leave it till later. And then when you log back in later, there's lots more, and it's suddenly extremely overwhelming. I have got a perfect or two perfect little antidotes for that. I do productivity sessions that have gone live today. Admin time-based sessions. They come as a pair. The first one that you do in the morning, it's got a coffee shop vibe. We're there for 15 minutes just to get through as many emails as you can first thing. And then the second one is for the evening, and we're going to meet together. Not really, but in like a virtual cocktail bar. It's nice and cozy. Get through your final batch of emails and admin so you can close your laptop and then get on with the rest of the evening. They're live right now in the Hot New Drops section of the Organize My Setup. We can do some admin together.

items.caption:
You’re going to looove this week’s drops 😍😍
✨ A kid’s bedroom declutter (for the kids to do!)
✨ Two admin bookend sessions. A 15 min morning coffee shop theme. Let’s sip lattes and get through those emails AND an evening end of the day cocktail bar theme … before you close your laptop we’ll get cosy and finish up the daily admin so you can start the morning with a clear inbox 🩷🩷

Not sure how to get started? Head over to The Organised Method app on the App Store and we’ll start you off with a 7 day free trial. Once in the app you’ll find all the guided productivity sessions in the TOM Rocks section ❤️ Let’s get productive!!!
items.transcript: So what is on your Tom to-do list next week? So July's theme is all about decluttering. Last week we had that mini declutter challenge. Tomorrow I am dropping a kid's room declutter for the kids to do. So you can literally just give them your phone and say, go and listen to that. It's 20 minutes. I will talk them through it. They can replay it as many times as they want to over the summer. It saves you from doing it. And then also, can't neglect the admin clutter either. On Wednesday, two sessions drop, two little admin sessions just to help you declutter your day, morning and an evening. Going to spend 15 minutes together going through your emails, making sure that we have dotted the I's and crossed the T's so that you can go to sleep and wake up without an admin hangover. Find them in the hot new drop section of the Organise Method app next week. And if you've never tried a guided productivity session, then you can try them for free for seven days. All you need to do is go to the app store, search for the Organise Method, and I will see you in the app.

items.caption: Fancy a declutter challenge to kick off July? 😍🎉
items.transcript: It's all about making running the house as easy as possible. What makes running the house easier? When there's less clutter. This week we have got a declutter challenge. Now this isn't though you have to take the whole week off work to declutter your house challenge. This is an intentional focused declutter challenge. A three-parter. You need to identify three areas, not whole rooms, just three key areas of your home that would just make it easier if there was less clutter. Think about things like a kid's wardrobe, maybe a playroom, maybe the kitchen island, dare I say it, maybe even the car. When things are less cluttered there's less stuff to tidy and there's less stuff to clean. They're gonna drop the first, the second, the third of July this week alongside your daily keep me company cleaning sessions to listen to whilst you are doing your organized method job. Don't forget you can try the app for free for seven days if you've never given us a go and I will see you in the app.

items.caption: If cleaning feels overwhelming Break. It. Down. That’s the whole premise that I built @the_organised_method on and it still rings true today ❤️❤️❤️❤️

It’s Sunday and you know what that means... 👀 New week preview time! Here’s what’s coming up in the app this week. 5 Bitesize Clean-alongs are task (living room bedroom hallway kitchen and bathroom) Want to get your home sorted FAST? 🙋 Drop a yes below! And I’ll see you in the app tomorrow - you’ll find the cleans under the HOT NEW DROPS section 🎉🎉🎉
items.transcript: How many times have you told yourself you were going to start a cleaning challenge and then just completely forgot to do it? This is a bite-sized cleaning challenge and each one of them is only 10 minutes long. Now, before you say to me, Gemma, I can't possibly get anything done in 10 minutes, let me say this. You'll get more done in 10 minutes than if you don't do anything at all. Each one focuses on a different room of your house and the way it's structured is you can either just do one and leave it at that or you can stack living room with kitchen or kitchen with bathroom or you can do all five of them together and then you have got a 50-minute house reset.
